title: Girth in Service of Purpose: The Thiccc Thing That Works
description: The Thiccctionary editorial board argues that the highest order of thicccness is not decorative but load-bearing, girth as the engine of purpose itself.

title: The Physics of Thiccc: Why Big Things Stay Big
description: Square-cube law, structural necessity, and the engineering logic behind why truly large objects look the way they do. A field guide to the geometry of girth.

title: The Bagger 288: The Thiccc-est Machine on Earth
description: At 96 metres tall and 220 metres long, the Bagger 288 is the largest land vehicle ever built. A field report on what it means to be truly, structurally, irreversibly thiccc.

title: Foods of Substance: A Thiccc Survey
description: From Parmigiano wheels to heritage tomatoes to Atlantic Giant pumpkins, when food's structural commitment becomes its identity.

title: The Anatomy of a Thiccc Vehicle
description: Five parts worth knowing: wheelbase, aft, dually, cargo bay, kin. A field guide to the parts of a thiccc vehicle.

title: Concrete: A Material Study
description: The material behind cement mixers, Brutalism, and Hoover Dam. Why concrete forces thicccness on everything it builds.

title: Thiccc Things You Walk Past Every Day
description: Fire hydrants, mailboxes, bollards, dumpsters, HVAC, the unsung architecture of a sidewalk.

title: A Taxonomy of Thiccc Architecture
description: Defense, ideology, and mass housing. Three programs, one silhouette. Castle walls, Brutalism, Khrushchyovka.

title: The Inherent Thicccness of Industrial Scale
description: Why physics insists on the silhouette. Bagger 288, Saturn V, supertankers, Hoover Dam.

title: Why Cement Trucks Are the Thiccc-est Industrial Vehicle
description: An argument, with engineering. The mixer is not in competition. It is the standard.

title: How to Spot a Thiccc Thing in the Wild: A Field Guide
description: Three tests. No instruments required. Silhouette, ratio, character.

title: The Five Thiccc-est Things We’ve Catalogued (So Far)
description: A working ranking. Heritage tomatoes to wide-body airliners. A hierarchy of girth.

title: What Counts as Thiccc? An Editorial Guide
description: The standing reference for what we’ll catalog and what we won’t. Includes the submission test.

title: A Brief History of Thiccc, the Word
description: From early-2000s message boards to mainstream dictionaries. With sources.
